name: The Nighthawk
tagline: Chicago-style comfort food in a historic Chinatown building — Italian beef sandwiches, Maxwell Street Polish, and late-night Kung Fu Ham.
mustOrder: Italian beef sandwich — Chicago-style, juicy and proper
Chicago-style hot dog — no ketchup, ever
Kung Fu Ham — deep-fried pork belly with Thai chili garlic
parking: Street parking on S King St; paid lots on Smith/Maunakea
talkStory: Opened by a Midwest transplant who brought authentic Chicago-style Italian beef sandwiches, Maxwell Street Polish, and proper Chicago dogs (no ketchup, ever) to a beautifully restored Chinatown building. The Kung Fu Ham — deep-fried pork belly with Thai chili garlic — is a house original that bridges Chicago grit and Honolulu creativity. Downtown workers fill it at lunch, the Chinatown bar crowd takes over late night. Open until midnight.
